issue_title: python 3 for `drep` but python 2 for `checkm`
issue_body: Based on looking at the `drep` python code and [Issue 22](https://github.com/MrOlm/drep/issues/22), it appears that drep requires python3. I don't see such a requirement specified in the `setup.py` file, but still, it appears to be python3 dependent. However, `drep` has `checkm` as a dependency, which is only compatible with python2. This has caused some issues with the bioconda drep recipe. It would be very helpful to make `drep` python2 compatible so that it can "play nicely" with checkm.

issue_title: support for form data
issue_body:
Testing HTML forms currently requires manually encoding the submitted data:

``` yaml
- name: create new item
  POST: /items
  request_headers:
      Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
  data: name=foo
```

This gets unwieldy as soon as there are multiple parameters and/or the data requires encoding:

``` yaml
  data: name=hello%20world&desc=l%C3%B6r%C3%A4m%20ips%C3%BCm&tag=foo&tag=bar&...
```

(that's still a fairly tame example)

This greatly impedes readability. It also seems inconsistent, given the support for structured data in `query_parameters` and JSON request bodies (the docs specifically mention that this allows "more flexibility when doing a `POST` or `PUT`" - there's no reason this affordance should be limited to JSON).

Since form submissions - or, more generally, `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` - is fairly common, even beyond browser-based applications (i.e. also used in APIs), gabbi should auto-encode form data (as determined by the `Content-Type` header):

``` yaml
  data:
      name: hello world
      desc: löräm ipsüm
      tag: 
      - foo
      - bar
```

(Arguably we might also want to support an ordered sequence of key-value pairs.)

issue_title: More sensible prefix + unit combinations
issue_body:
As it is now, pint is pretty liberal about combining prefixes + units.  For example, "pinch" is parsed as a "picoinch", which doesn't seem very useful to me.  Does anyone have any thoughts on supporting more fine-grained prefix + unit name combinations?  It would be helpful when parsing unverified input to have more restrictive unit names.  We could allow prefix combinations to be specified in the definitions file, so people could write their own rules.

Possible rules:
1) SI prefixes only with SI units (no "pinch" like above)
2) binary prefixes only with information units ("kibibyte" is useful, "kibimeter" less so)
3) abbreviated prefixes only with abbreviated units (allow "km" but no "kmeter")
4) no prefixes when a unit name has one built in (disallow "kilokph")

Comments?  Dissenting opinions?

issue_title: wraps for functions with parameters in arbitrary quantities
issue_body:
Hello,

I've found pint quite nice and wanted to adopt it for our project, but then I failed to wrap my functions.
The following two test cases, added to test_unit.TestRegistry.test_wraps, fail:

```
    f4 = ureg.wraps(10*ureg.uA, [None, ], strict=False)(func)
    self.assertEqual(f4(3.), 30 * ureg.uA)

    f5 = ureg.wraps(10*ureg.uA, [5*ureg.uA, ], strict=False)(func)
    self.assertEqual(f5(10*ureg.uA), 20 * ureg.uA)
```

How can one achieve such wrapping?

If this is not possible I probably can fix it and provide a pull request.

Best, Roland

    do_not_fragment = self.packet.off & dpkt.ip.IP_DF

issue_title: EEP -data remaining when creating multiple packets
issue_body:
The EEP-object is only loaded once (i.e. when first initializing the `Packet` -class). It's designed this way, as it includes the loading of `EEP.xml`, which in the future is quite resource intensive, being full of profiles ;)
Because of this, EEP-class shouldn't retain any data.  Currently it holds `_profile` and `_data_description`, which are improperly used, and can therefore cause unexpected behavior.

The issue is demonstrated by the test in commit b26fbc9f23c9caed648a528b82371a68304a04d0.

issue_title: Make ks.comment available in mappy.fastx_reader
issue_body:
Enhancement.
Fastest python reader I know.
```
diff --git a/python/mappy.pyx b/python/mappy.pyx
index 1f17dc0..11e0b11 100644
--- a/python/mappy.pyx
+++ b/python/mappy.pyx
@@ -154,8 +154,9 @@ def fastx_read(fn):
                if ks.qual.l > 0: qual = ks.qual.s if isinstance(ks.qual.s, str) else ks.qual.s.decode()
                else: qual = None
                name = ks.name.s if isinstance(ks.name.s, str) else ks.name.s.decode()
+               comment = ks.comment.s if isinstance(ks.comment.s, str) else ks.comment.s.decode()
                seq = ks.seq.s if isinstance(ks.seq.s, str) else ks.seq.s.decode()

issue_title: Characteristic write and bytearray size max
issue_body: gatttool supports a 20 byte payload for BLE, but the bytearray used in the char_write_handle() seems to max out at 17 bytes. If I make the byte array 18 bytes or larger I get "SyntaxError: invalid token." I have verified using gatttool in linux and sending 20 byte packets with not problem.  I have noticed using the linux gatttool that if the value you want to write has "0x" pre-appended, the gatttool will insert an extra 00 byte and you will loose a byte. Not sure why it's short 3 bytes in this case from the 20 byte max.

issue_title: dont support unittest.skipIf
issue_body:
version:   2.7.2
os : mac

code；

```python
from __future__ import unicode_literals
import sys
import unittest


class TestStates(unittest.TestCase):

    def test0Pass(self):
        """
        This test will print output to stdout, and then pass.
        """
        print("Sunshine and daisies")

    @unittest.skipIf(True, "I am skipIf++")
    def test1Fail(self):
        """
        This test will print output to stderr, and then fail an assertion.
        """
        sys.stderr.write("Doom and gloom.\n")
        self.assertTrue(False)

    @unittest.skipUnless(False,"I am skipUnless")
    def test2Error(self):
        """
        An Exception will be raised (and not caught) while running this test.
        """
        raise Exception

    @unittest.skip("This is the 'reason' portion of the skipped test.")
    def test3Skip(self):
        """
        This test will be skipped.
        """
        pass

    @unittest.expectedFailure
    def test4ExpectedFailure(self):
        """
        This test will fail, but we expect it to.
        """
        self.assertEqual(True, False)

    @unittest.expectedFailure
    def test5UnexpectedPass(self):
        """
        This test will pass, but we expected it to fail!
        """
        pass
```

issue_title: Feature Request in Log Levels
issue_body:
PROTOCOL, NETWORK, EXTENDED log levels capture all the values, including encoded password which can be easily decoded. This creates a security concern of knowing other users password, for those who have access to the logs. This creates a hesitation in enabling this log level in a production environment, even for the debugging. 

Whereas, ERROR, BASIC log levels doesn't capture any user information which makes it difficult to related which log was for which account.

Its a feature request.

Either removed the encoded password value capture when `ldap3.utils.log.set_library_log_hide_sensitive_data` is set to `True` even when the log level is set to PROTOCOL, NETWORK, EXTENDED.
Or include basic details such as user DN or CN in the ERROR, BASIC log levels.

issue_title: visualisation of (undefined/external) agents and activities
issue_body:
Agents are rendered in a pentagon/house shape, and activities as a rectangle. If now an agent/activity is not defined/declared within a PROV document, but is external (e.g. defined in a previous PROV document), then the dot graph is topographically still rendered correctly (with edges and the undeclared element rendered in grey), however the shape of the element is an oval (that of an entity).

I suppose that by the context (the type of an edge, e.g. through the `wasAttributedTo` or the `wasGeneratedBy` edges), the `prov` library may be able to declare the shape of the vertex for graph rendering with dot consistently.

Or is there something I'm missing, thus making the rendered graph and its behaviour correct in this context with undeclared vertices?

issue_title: heredoc parsing fails if file has DOS/Windows line-endings (carriage returns)
issue_body:
heredoc parsing fails if file has DOS/Windows line-endings (carriage returns)

Sample TF file, with heredoc notation (<<EOT):
```
$ cat test.tf 
resource "vault_generic_secret" "example" {
  path = "secret/foo"

  data_json = <<EOT
{
  "foo":   "bar",
  "pizza": "cheese"
}
EOT
}
```

Unix line-endings work:
```
$ dos2unix test.tf 
dos2unix: converting file test.tf to Unix format ...

$ echo -e "import hcl\nwith open('test.tf','r') as fp: obj = hcl.load(fp)" | python
```

DOS line-endings don't:
```
$ unix2dos test.tf
unix2dos: converting file test.tf to DOS format ...

$ echo -e "import hcl\nwith open('test.tf','r') as fp: obj = hcl.load(fp)" | python
Traceback (most recent call last):

issue_title: Multipage Support
issue_body:
I am working on adding support for a 'multi page' app, that would allow for defining routes to different spyre "apps".

You can check out my progress at my fork/master. I've basically got it working. I've got a draft of an example app (that is the example_show_all_the_inputs.py, with 2 additional app classes that are basically exact copies of the first.) At the bottom you can see what I've got for how you would use this functionality.

I added an additional class in server.py that I'm calling Site, that takes the top node as a required argument and exposes a methods to add a new page/route, to change the top node (which would be somewhat dangerous, but there might be a use case to do so), and to launch the site.

I'm not really happy with the naming conventions, as I'm calling the top node the "root" node, which is a little confusing because the object is a server.Root object. Anyone have any input on a better naming convention for a page/route/node and for the top of the page/node/route tree?

There is also an issue with this setup that I think has something to do with how the spinning_wheel is implemented. While the spinning wheel is active the terminal throws a warning/error "QPixmap: It is not safe to use pixmaps outside the GUI thread". My guess is that whatever is being used to determine when the spinning wheel should be replaced by a plot or HTML element is causing this error. It's not strictly causing any problems, but it's probably something that would need to be figured out before incorporating this kind of functionality into your master.

In general what are your thoughts on this?

In terms of how this might be used, I'm not sure if there is anything that could/should be done to the template to allow better use of a multipage site (A navbar across the top? An easy ability to make the control panel buttons be links? an output template/function?). It would be easy enough to just make an HTML output block that would have links, but I'm just wondering if this is something that maybe could be supported better by updating the view.html template.

issue_title: nodeenv --list is raising TypeError
issue_body:
When installed globally with `sudo pip install nodeenv`, `nodeenv --list` raises an exception.

This is due to `pkg_resources.parse_version` method having different return types in older versions. 

![nodeenv_bug](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/6031925/6599860/d253a2b0-c814-11e4-94d6-9a9d79cdf27c.png)


Possible hotfix solutions are:
* write a helper function to parse strings,
* write our own Version type :-1: 

But atm I'm working on finding out which version of `setuptools` introduced the newer (currently used in master) Version type that parses version strings and rely on it if `setuptools` is >= that version.

I'll combine that with one of two approaches:
* the raw string representation we get with regex pattern. However ascending sorting will be `0.1.0, 0.10.0, 0.9.0`,
*  the before mentioned helper function


PR will be submitted soon-ish. :8ball:

issue_title: as_dict does not return pure dicts
issue_body:
Hi there,

I recently started to use psutil - and it is awesome 👍.
Especially the as_dict function is very useful. I try to benchmark some functions and I call the as_dict, bound on the process before and after the function to get my measurements. As the as_dict returns a dict, I wanted to store it via dataset [dataset.readthedocs.io/en/latest/] in a inmemory sqlite database, so that I can write it to disk after the benchmark and do not interfere with the disk I/O. However, dataset only accepts true dicts, while as_dict gives back a dict with named_tuples nested in it (i.e. io_counters, memory_info, memory_full_info, etc) and lists (connections, open_files).
At the moment I am thinking of converting these named_tuples and lists into json so that this degraded dict becomes one which can be handled with sqlite / dataset. Is there anything planed to change the behavior of as_dict or would there be an better way of getting it into some compatible format as I described? :)

Thanks a lot!

Why I can't see usernames (e.g SYSTEM) of some processes with _psutil.process_iter()_ while I can with powershell for example?

Thank you in advance for your help,

issue_title: Process.memory_maps() does not return addresses above 32 bits on 64 bit builds
issue_body:
Using the distributed x64 version of psutil and running against a 64 bit process with memory regions higher than 0xffffffff, all bits higher than 32 are truncated. 

Example:
Running a 64 bit process under x64dbg, I have msctf.dll mapped in at  0x000007FEFF020000.

If I get the memory map for this process using memory_map, the entry corresponding to msctf.dll is shown with base address 0xFF020000, cutting off the highest 32 bits of the address.

issue_title: Does freezegun support threading.Timer and concurrent.futures.Future triggers and timeouts?
issue_body:
We've been mocking time in Windows to coordinate datetime.now, time.sleep, threading.Timer triggers and concurrent.futures.Future timeouts. None of it works on Linux--likely due to different internals that don't trigger the mocks. People bring up freezegun, but we wanted to check if that's viable for handling this stuff.

I already see that time.sleep() doesn't get mocked by default, but we can route that ourselves. However, I am wondering about the rest. If freezegun is managing time, will it:

1. Start a function scheduled by threading.Timer when its delay is reached?
2. Time out a Future.result() call if the timeout threshold is reached?

issue_title: Recommend Brotli instead of brotlipy?
issue_body:
When Brotli support was first added to WhiteNoise, if I remember correctly, the official brotli repo did not yet have its own Python package/bindings. As such, the WhiteNoise docs recommend using [brotlipy](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/brotlipy).

However there's now the official Python package, [Brotli](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/Brotli), which I've been using with WhiteNoise for a while.

Unless there are good reasons to stick with the unofficial bindings, I think we should update the WhiteNoise docs to point people at the `Brotli` package instead.

issue_title: [WEB UI] Flood if refresh_time is negative

We flood the server to refresh The webapp should check the refresh delay and if that is < 1s, set to 1s or 5s. #### Versions * Glances (glances -V): latest develop version * PSutil (glances -V): * Operating System (lsb_release -a): #### Logs You can also pastebin the Glances logs file (https://glances.readthedocs.io/en/latest/config.html#logging)

issue_title: support SSL client auth in stompest.async
issue_body:
I'm attempting to authenticate to STOMP on ActiveMQ that requires SSL clients to present a x509 keypair in order to connect.

For the stompest sync client, it is really simple, I just have to provide the public cert and key to my ssl context with `load_cert_chain()`:

```python
context = ssl.create_default_context()
context.load_cert_chain(certfile="kdreyer.pem", keyfile='kdreyer.key')
...
CONFIG = StompConfig(BROKER, sslContext=context)
```
... and then I can receive messages in my queue, etc.


Unfortunately this does not work for the stompest async client. Here's the error I'm getting
```
INFO:stompest.async.protocol:Connecting to server.example.com:61612 ...
DEBUG:stompest.async.protocol:Sending CONNECT frame [version=1.0]
Unhandled error in Deferred:

INFO:stompest.async.listener:Disconnected: [('SSL routines', 'ssl3_read_bytes', 'sslv3 alert bad certificate')]
ERROR:stompest.async.listener:Disconnect because of failure: Unexpected connection loss [[('SSL routines', 'ssl3_read_bytes', 'sslv3 alert bad certificate')]]
DEBUG:stompest.async.listener:Calling disconnected errback: Unexpected connection loss [[('SSL routines', 'ssl3_read_bytes', 'sslv3 alert bad certificate')]]
```

I've been looking over [Twisted's docs for Client cert auth](https://twistedmatrix.com/documents/current/core/howto/ssl.html#client-with-certificates), but I'm a bit lost as to where I would set those options in stompest.async. Somewhere in `util.py` ?

issue_title: Command line arguments do not override config file values
issue_body:
If the following scheme is used and arguments are specified both in a config-file and from command line 
the correct priority is not always followed.
E.g. if we specify --testarg in the config-file and --no-testarg in the command line the result will be  testarg=True. This is due to the fact that --testarg and --no-testarg are treated as distinct arguments even if they refer to the same destination.

```
parser.add(
        '--testarg', dest='testarg', default=None, required=False,
        action='store_true', 
        help=help_handlers_activate % HANDLERS[handler]
    )
parser.add(
        '--no-testarg', dest='testarg', default=None, required=False,
        action='store_false', 
        help=help_handlers_deactivate % HANDLERS[handler]
    )
```

issue_title: String right truncation pyodbc.ProgrammingError when using `fast_executemany`
issue_body:
### Environment

To diagnose, we usually need to know the following, including version numbers.  On Windows, be
sure to specify 32-bit Python or 64-bit:

- Python: 3.6.3
- pyodbc: 4.0.23
- OS: Debian Stretch
- DB: SQL Server 
- driver: Microsoft ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server libmsodbcsql-17.0.so.1.1

### Issue

When executing a parameterized "UPDATE" statement with `fast_executemany`, the following exception is thrown.

```
ERROR - encoding with 'utf-16le' codec failed (SystemError: <built-in function utf_16_le_encode> returned a result with an error set)              
pyodbc.ProgrammingError: ('String data, right truncation: length 666 buffer 510', 'HY000')
```

The column widths in the target database have more than enough size to hold the data. 
This seems equivalent to Issue #337  except that in this case, I'm using 4.0.23 of pyodbc and MS ODBC Driver 17, which fixed the issue in that post but continue to be a problem for me.

I've trimmed down the data to just two rows and can consistently reproduce the problem with just those two rows. 

I'm using SQL Alchemy to construct the statements and am using the `connection.execute(stmt, [row1, row2])` method to execute.

Turning `fast_executemany` off fixes the problem, but unacceptably impacts performance.

issue_title: xmlchemy - ZeroOrMoreChoice
issue_body:
Hi!

(If this is not the place to send this message, and there's a mailing list or something, please let me know)

My company has a requirement to draw arbitrary polygons on PPT slides, and I was hoping to add support for custGeom to get this done. I'm also hoping to contribute this back in the future, if I can hopefully get close to the standards you've set forth.

That being said, I'm looking at ECMA-376, 4th edition - the Office Open XML File Formats spec. Section 20.1.9.8 summarizes the custGeom, which requires a pathLst element. The pathLst element contains zero or more path elements. The CT_Path2D element, as defined on page 4013 (!!!) of the spec (or line 2057 of section A.4.1, the drawing ML stuff) contains:

    <xsd:choice minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded">

...and a list of choices after that. The CT_Path2D element usually contains elements that starts with a moveTo, then a set of lnTo elements, and finally (optionally) a close element.

Does that require a new class in xmlchemy.py for ZeroOrMoreChoice, or is there already something there that I'm missing that would handle this use case? If so, are there any obvious pitfalls you would warn me about before I get into it?

Very much appreciated!

issue_title: Allow varargs when using only keyword arguments
issue_body:
If a test function is wrapped by another decorator, it may be hard to avoid varargs. However, it should still be safe to use `@given` if no positional arguments are used.

I have experimented with this by modifying `run_test_with_generator` to do the following instead of the current vararg check:
```python
        if generator_arguments and original_argspec.varargs:
            raise InvalidArgument(
                'varargs are not supported with positional arguments to @given'
            )
```

`tox -e py26,py27` passes locally for me, so I have reasonable confidence that this doesn't break anything obvious. If you're okay with this change, I'm happy to submit a pull request with the necessary tests and such.

issue_title: verification does not use root macaroon signature in bind
issue_body:
In the original macaroons paper, PrepareForRequest binds the signature of all the discharge macaroons to the root macaroon's signature. Pymacaroons does not do that when verifying macaroons more than one level deep - instead it binds the signature of the discharge macaroon to that of its parent. This is different (and incompatible) behaviour from that expected by libmacaroons and the Go macaroons implementation.

The following code demonstrates the issue. It uses both libmacaroons and pymacaroons to demonstrate the incompatibility, but the issue can adequately be demonstrated with pymacaroons alone.

	import pymacaroons
	import base64
	import json
	import macaroons
	
	def testpymacaroons():
	    print('test pymacaroons')
	    # Create a root macaroon with discharge.
	    root = pymacaroons.Macaroon(location="", identifier="root-id", key="root-key")
	    root.add_third_party_caveat("bob", "bob-caveat-root-key", "bob-is-great")
	    
	    # Create a discharge macaroon that requires a secondary discharge.
	    discharge1 = pymacaroons.Macaroon(location="bob", identifier="bob-is-great", key="bob-caveat-root-key")
	    discharge1.add_third_party_caveat("barbara", "barbara-caveat-root-key", "barbara-is-great")
	    
	    # Create the secondary discharge macaroon.
	    discharge2 = pymacaroons.Macaroon(location="barbara", identifier="barbara-is-great", key="barbara-caveat-root-key")
	    
	    # Prepare the discharge macaroons for request.
	    discharge1 = root.prepare_for_request(discharge1)
	    discharge2 = root.prepare_for_request(discharge2)
	    
	    # verification fails with MacaroonInvalidSignatureException.
	    try:
	        pymacaroons.Verifier().verify(root, "root-key", [discharge1, discharge2])
	        print('pymacaroons verifed ok')
	    except Exception as e:
	        print('pymacaroons verification failed: {}'.format(e))

	    # libmacaroons succeeds in verifying the same
	    # macaroons that pymacaroons failed on.
	    l_root = macaroons.deserialize(bytes(root.serialize()))
	    l_discharge1 = macaroons.deserialize(bytes(discharge1.serialize()))
	    l_discharge2 = macaroons.deserialize(bytes(discharge2.serialize()))
	
	    try:
	        macaroons.Verifier().verify(l_root, "root-key", [l_discharge1, l_discharge2])
	        print('libmacaroons verified ok after deserialization')
	    except Exception as e:
	        print('libmacaroons verification failed: {}'.format(e))
	
	def testlibmacaroons():
	    print('test libmacaroons')
	    # Create a root macaroon with discharge.
	    root = macaroons.create("", "root-key", "root-id")
	    root = root.add_third_party_caveat("bob", "bob-caveat-root-key", "bob-is-great")
	    
	    # Create a discharge macaroon that requires a secondary discharge.
	    discharge1 = macaroons.create("bob", "bob-caveat-root-key", "bob-is-great")
	    discharge1 = discharge1.add_third_party_caveat("babara", "barbara-caveat-root-key", "barbara-is-great")
	    
	    # Create the secondary discharge macaroon.
	    discharge2 = macaroons.create("barbara", "barbara-caveat-root-key", "barbara-is-great")
	    
	    # Prepare the discharge macaroons for request.
	    discharge1 = root.prepare_for_request(discharge1)
	    discharge2 = root.prepare_for_request(discharge2)

	    # verification succeeds ok.
	    macaroons.Verifier().verify(root, "root-key", [discharge1, discharge2])
	    
	    print('libmacaroons verified ok')

	    # pymacaroons fails to verify the macaroons that libmacaroons succeeded
	    # in verifying
	    p_root = pymacaroons.Macaroon.deserialize(root.serialize(format=1))
	    p_discharge1 = pymacaroons.Macaroon.deserialize(discharge1.serialize(format=1))
	    p_discharge2 = pymacaroons.Macaroon.deserialize(discharge2.serialize(format=1))
	
	    try:
	        pymacaroons.Verifier().verify(p_root, "root-key", [p_discharge1, p_discharge2])
	        print('pymacaroons verified ok after deserialization')
	    except Exception as e:
	        print('pymacaroons verification failed: {}'.format(e))
	
	testpymacaroons()
	testlibmacaroons()

issue_title: Workbook.close sets self.fileclosed before closing the file and thus cannot be retried
issue_body:
I've frequently had the problem that, at the end of my script, when I try and close the workbook I've accidentally left the excel file I'm writing to open after checking the output is correct and it then fails with a permission error. I tried to solve this by introducing a loop with a try..except to catch the PermissionError but this doesn't work because in workbook.close it has these lines:
```Python
    if not self.fileclosed:
        self.fileclosed = 1
        self._store_workbook()
```
which mean that if `self._store_workbook()` fails then it's already set the `self.fileclosed` flag and won't try again. I believe it should be like this instead:
```Python
    if not self.fileclosed:
        self._store_workbook()
        self.fileclosed = 1
```
so that you can retry if the `self._store_workbook()` call fails. I have locally modified my copy and this appears to be functional.

issue_title: Missing tag pair using chess.pgn.read_game() or chess.pgn.scan_headers()
issue_body:
According to [pgn standard spec](http://www.saremba.de/chessgml/standards/pgn/pgn-complete.htm#c8.1) tag pair names can contain underscores, but python-chess pgn parser ignores such names.

```
[Event "Local event"]
[Site "London"]
[Date "2000.01.01"]
[Round "1"]
[White "First Player"]
[Black "Second Player"]
[Result "1/2-1/2"]
[1Underscored_Tag "tag value"]

1. e4 c5 1/2-1/2
```

Telling the truth I'v never met any .pgn containing nor digits nor underscores in any tag, but the standard enables it, so it's better to handle it.

issue_title: Use of isinstance within wrap_valid provides inconsistent ValueError
issue_body:
```python
with wrap_valid('x', x) as v:
    v.alid = isinstance(x, int) and x >= 0
```

Always raises a `ValueError` in case of invalid data, whether x=-1 (value) or x='foo' (type).
We should provide an answer, here are some possible ways
 * provide an alternate checkinstance(x, int) method to raise a subclass of TypeError and demonstrate it in the examples
 * make extensive use of source code introspection in wrap_valid to detect that isinstance was used... not fond of it and it will probably always fail in some cases (starting with terminal input on windows, where we cant inspect the source today)

issue_body:
This is more of a behaviour question. If there are multiple processes(A and B) each running a scheduler  with `sqlalchemy` based job store using the same database. And an `interval`(-trigger) based job is submitted to only scheduler from ProcessA. Will the scheduler from a ProcessB be able to schedule its execution to its own executor?

If scheduler from ProcessB `wakesup` (for any reason) only then it will be able to schedule it. 

This seems more like a race condition (?)

The file size of the sqlite3 database is about 45 MB, which I got after about one week of training using a corpus size of ~78 MB. I am trying to get a response from the trained model and it is almost one and half hours and still I didn't get any response. Here is the code for training my data based on a corpus in the folder /data

```
from chatterbot.trainers import ChatterBotCorpusTrainer, Trainer
from chatterbot import ChatBot

bot = ChatBot(
    "Remedy",
    storage_adapter='chatterbot.storage.SQLStorageAdapter',
    #input_adapter = 'chatterbot.input.TerminalAdapter',
    #output_adapter = 'chatterbot.output.TerminalAdapter',
    logic_adapters=[
        {
            "import_path": "chatterbot.logic.BestMatch",
            "statement_comparison_function": "chatterbot.comparisons.levenshtein_distance",
            "response_selection_method": "chatterbot.response_selection.get_first_response"
        },
{
            'import_path': 'chatterbot.logic.LowConfidenceAdapter',
            'threshold': 0.65,
            'default_response': 'I am sorry, but I do not understand.'
        }

)

Can someone figure out the problem, why it takes really long to get a response (already waited close to 2 hours).

issue_title: Can't connect to Jenkins HTTPS with self-signed certificate
issue_body:
I've set up a Jenkins instance over HTTPS with a self-signed certificate, and I cannot get jenkins-webapi to connect with the self-signed cert. issue_title: export csv which include Chinese with python3 running under windows
issue_body:
File "test.py", line 322, in fkn_export
    worksheet.export(filename = filename)
  File "C:\Users\spff\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python36-32\lib\site-packages\pygsheets\worksheet.py", line 833, in export
    writer.writerows(self.get_all_values())
UnicodeEncodeError: 'cp950' codec can't encode character '\u7a4f' in position 63: illegal multibyte sequence



with the line 831, my Windows is opening a file using cp950(extension of Big5), and will make anything writing encode to cp950.

I'm pretty sure it can be fixed by changing the line 831 to 
`with open(ifilename, 'wt', encode='utf-8') as f:`

and I think it'll be better to make the encoding a parameter(default is utf8 or whatever) for export() and its callers to let users make their choice.

issue_title: Django Rest Framework static assets return 404
issue_body: I am using the serverless-wsgi with a Django Rest Framework project. Using the browsable API functionality of DRF, when I run the local server using ```sls wsgi serve -p 8000```, I get loads of 404 errors for the rest framework's static files. When I run the local server using ```./manage.py runserver```, I get no 404 errors. EDIT: Not a big deal, since I am not going to use the browsable api functionality in production anyways, but it's great for testing locally See the contrasting screenshots below

issue_title: Performance of searching for oldest message
issue_body:
I run the following filter
`msgs = account.inbox.all().filter(datetime_received__gt=start_date).filter(is_read=False).order_by('datetime_received')`

and the expected results are returned quickly. 

But when I try to access the oldest message with
`oldest_msg = msg[0]`

then the execution time depends greatly on whether the oldest message is a few messages ago, or if it is hundreds of messages ago. 

For example, if there are two unread messages and both are recent, the time is less than a second. But if there two unread messages are many messages apart, then the time can be 30s

Is there a better approach to retrieving oldest messages? I've tried using a slice [:10] of all the msgs, I've considered using Only. But I think there must be some approach I've missed.

issue_title: Folders with meeting requests causes KeyError exceptions.
issue_body:
Trying to use the library to fetch unread items from a mailbox. Ran into an issue where if the folder contains meeting requests a KeyError exception occurs.

Simple test:

``` python
items = account.inbox.filter(is_read=False)
for item in items:
    print('subject: {}, read: {}'.format(item.subject, item.is_read))
```

Example error message:

``` python
<exchangelib.queryset.QuerySet object at 0x7f134dfec7f0>
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "ews.py", line 30, in <module>
    for item in items:
  File "/home/alex/src/django-dashing-test/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/exchangelib/queryset.py", line 125, in __iter__
    self._cache = list(self._query())
  File "/home/alex/src/django-dashing-test/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/exchangelib/queryset.py", line 97, in _query
    items = self.folder.fetch(ids=ids, only_fields=additional_fields)
  File "/home/alex/src/django-dashing-test/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/exchangelib/folders.py", line 1585, in fetch
    return self.account.fetch(folder=self, *args, **kwargs)
  File "/home/alex/src/django-dashing-test/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/exchangelib/account.py", line 312, in fetch
    items
  File "/home/alex/src/django-dashing-test/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/exchangelib/account.py", line 311, in <lambda>
    lambda i: validation_folder.item_model_from_tag(i.tag).from_xml(elem=i, account=self, folder=folder),
  File "/home/alex/src/django-dashing-test/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/exchangelib/folders.py", line 1419, in item_model_from_tag
    return cls.ITEM_MODEL_MAP[tag]
KeyError: '{http://schemas.microsoft.com/exchange/services/2006/types}MeetingRequest'
```

As far as I understood it from the source no MeetingRequest class has not been implemented yet. Is there a reason why it's missing or have I missed something?

I hacked together a bare minimum version of the various meeting requests types and got it working. I could implement more of the meeting types and create a pull request, just wanted to verify if I'm on the right track?

Example implementation:

``` python
class MeetingRequest(ItemMixIn):
    # Supported attrs: https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/office/aa565229(v=exchg.150).aspx
    ELEMENT_NAME = 'MeetingRequest'
    FIELDURI_PREFIX = 'meetingrequest' # educated guess
    ITEM_FIELDS = {
        'from': ('From', Mailbox),
        'is_read': ('IsRead', bool),
        'start': ('Start', EWSDateTime),
        'end': ('End', EWSDateTime),
    }
    ORDERED_FIELDS = (
        'subject', 'from', 'is_read', 'start', 'end'
    )
    REQUIRED_FIELDS = {'subject'}
    READONLY_FIELDS = {'from'}

    __slots__ = tuple(ITEM_FIELDS) + tuple(Item.ITEM_FIELDS)

    def __init__(self, **kwargs):
        for k in self.ITEM_FIELDS:
            field_type = self.ITEM_FIELDS[k][1]
            default = False if (k in self.required_fields() and field_type == bool) else None
            v = kwargs.pop(k, default)
            setattr(self, k, v)
        super().__init__(**kwargs)
```

Unicode filename gets crippled when drag-and-dropped
issue_body: When a Python script is run by drag-and-dropping another file, its path gets crippled in `sys.argv` if it contains Unicode characters, and our `unicode_argv` module doesn't resolve this. Note that this is an issue even in Python 3. Since even calling `GetCommandLineW` returns a crippled path, this may be issue with Windows itself. I have started an issue in Python bug tracker: http://bugs.python.org/issue27469.

issue_title: Explicitly create Threads instead of implicitly?
issue_body:
Threading is a complicated subject and it can lead to difficult to track bugs.
By default TeleBot creates a new Thread for each registered listener and each registered message handler. Now, if two message handlers or two listeners try to access the same (not thread-safe) resource, the programmer has got a problem. So unless the programmer is aware that this library creates Threads (and knows how to handle them (locks, semaphores, queues)), Threads can be a threat ( :) ) to the stability of bots.
My proposal: 
Create an optional ```create_threads``` parameter for the TeleBot constructor, which defaults to ```False```.
Check for this parameter when calling listeners or message handlers:
```python
if self.create_threads:
    t = threading.Thread(target=message_handler, args=message)
    t.start()
else:
    message_handler(message)
```

The drawback of this is of course program speed. The bot will block until a listener or message handler has returned. Until then the bot cannot receive new messages. But my estimation is that this drawback is less important than the overhead (for the, especially novice, programmer) of creating Threads. With an optional argument, the programmer can decide for himself whether he wants to go down the dangerous road of multithreading.
Is my reasoning correct?

issue_title: Accept versions with a 'v' prefix
issue_body:
A common convention I have seen across various projects is to prefix the semantic version string with a `v` (e.g. `v1.2.3`). I was wondering if there would be interest in supporting it here. I would believe the scope to be fairly limited (allow users to specify strings prefixed with `v` when instantiating and make the `str` of the `Version` class include a `v` prefix, if appropriate). 

I respect this deviates from the semver standards and would understand if you declined the request. I was unsure how you were balancing strictly following the semver standards versus user convenience in this library.

The development team at my employer uses this library a ton and has adapted the current version to accept `v` prefixes. We would be willing to share our implementation if there was an appetite for supporting this customization, but are, of course, open to other implementations and suggestions.

Thank you again for all of your work on this project. We have found it very useful and use it almost everywhere.

issue_title: test_loss.test_bce_loss has fixed seed that can mask flakiness
issue_body:
The unit test in title have been using fixed seed to mask flakiness. Suggested action:
1. Evaluate whether the test is flaky without fixed seed. If not, remove seed. Else move to 2
2. If test is flaky, determine whether it's an actual uncaught edge case. If so, fix the operator. Else move to 3
3. If numerical instability is inevitable, adjust tolerance level appropriately.

issue_body:
## Description
when passing numpy arrays the dtype of the numpy arrays is not used to construct mxnet ndarrays.
Please see https://discuss.mxnet.io/t/integer-labels-in-ndarrayiter/169

## Environment info (Required)
Deep Learning AMI, mxnet master, HEAD: f9e25b47c0faec07e940fedb718df2f38dd925b4

Package used (Python/R/Scala/Julia): Python

MXNet commit hash: f9e25b47c0faec07e940fedb718df2f38dd925b4

## Error Message: No error but the dtype of Y_train is not used

## Steps to reproduce

Run the following:
```
import mxnet as mx
import numpy as np


X_train = np.array([1,2,3,4,5])
Y_train = np.array([1,2,3,4,5111111122], dtype='int64')

train_data = mx.io.NDArrayIter(X_train, Y_train, 2, shuffle=False)

print train_data.label[0]
```



## What have you tried to solve it?

The issue is that the dtype of numpy is not passed when calling the array util (https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/master/python/mxnet/io.py#L509)
The workaround can be to just use nd arrays for Y_train instead of numpy ndarray. It fixes the issue but with shuffle=True this again breaks. (https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/master/python/mxnet/io.py#L634)

Is there any specific reason to not pass the dtype of numpy or ndarrays to array util ?

issue_title: Improve efficiency of reorder_view
issue_body:
The `reorder_view` calls save on each model. Since it is dealing with a set of `ids` it could be more efficient to use `update`.

Based on #13 the algorithm could be:

* Find the min and max of the items being reordered.
* Check that the items being reordered fit between the min and max.
* Move items being reordered to the end of the list.
* Update items to be consecutive numbers between min and max.

issue_title: Riak Returns Wrong Value for Key after PB request timeout happens [JIRA: CLIENTS-710]
issue_body:
This bug happens with the following flow.

A user gets a Connection object from the pool
1) sends Request X
2) waits for Response X
3) timeout happens, and dealt with at the application level

At this stage, the connection is released back in the pool, the next user comes along.
4) sends Request Y
6) receives Response X, because it was received in the meanwhile.

Some cases, the library throws: "'unexpected protocol buffer message code" if the requests were of different kinds.

Worst case, if the requests were both of the type "Get" but for a different key, you will ask for key X, but get value of Y.

We ended up with some corrupted data because of this.

To reproduce:

```
(2.7)➜  ~  virtualenv ~/riakbug
(2.7)➜  ~  source ~/riakbug/bin/activate
(riakbug)➜  ~  pip install riak
(riakbug)➜  ~  pip freeze
...
riak==2.3.0
...

(riakbug)➜  ~  python
Python 2.7.10 (default, Jul 14 2015, 19:46:27)

>>> from riak import RiakClient

>>> rc = RiakClient(protocol='pbc', host="172.17.8.101", pb_port=8087, transport_options={"timeout":0.01})
# you might have to play around with the timeout value to get the conditions right

>>> rc.ping()
True

>>> rc.get_buckets()
Traceback (most recent call last):
....
socket.timeout: timed out

>>> rc.bucket("a_bucket").get("a_key")
...
riak.riak_error.RiakError: 'unexpected protocol buffer message code: 16, <riak.pb.riak_kv_pb2.RpbListBucketsResp object at 0x10d442f50>'
# NOTE: that this is the response to the first request to get_buckets.

>>> rc.bucket("a_bucket").get("another_key")
<riak.riak_object.RiakObject object at 0x10d440b10>
# NOTE: This is the value of a_key, not of another_key

```

What could possibly be done about it is that when a timeout happens, the connection could be marked as a BadResource, and hence removed from the connection pool.
Another option for this specific problem is to flush any incoming data before making a new request.

This same issue could possibly also happen when other errors are thrown in step 3 above, not just for socket.timeout.

issue_title: Latest version not performing Euclidean Search ?
issue_body:
Hi,

I've been running a version of Annoy that is a few months old on my Mac up until a couple of days ago when I re-installed. I'm now getting near neighbour searches for Euclidean distance that are different to those I got with the old version. They seem to be the results you'd expect from an angular distance search. I ran a brute-force KNN on the same data, and sure enough - the old Annoy lib gets pretty much the same as a KNN euclidean search. The new version doesn't. Could something have broken with the Euclidean search when the Manhattan search was added ?

I'm calling it with:

myIndex = AnnoyIndex(myData, metric="euclidean")

...but the data it returns when queried looks like cosine/angular distance comparison.

I put print statement in the __init__.py to check the metric variable is being passed, and it's certainly getting that far. Can't tell what's happening inside the compiled library though.

I'll dig around in the C code, but my C is so rusty I doubt I'd be able to spot a bug if there's one there.

Ian

Now, when requesting the branch's tracking branch with `Head(...).tracking_branch()`, it doesn't remove the quotes and returns something like `origin/"refs/heads/feature#123"`.

Example `.git/config` file:

```
[core]
	repositoryformatversion = 0
	filemode = true
	bare = false
	logallrefupdates = true
	ignorecase = true
	precomposeunicode = true
[remote "origin"]
	url = /var/folders/n8/s89552kd2s9_v1lcgbgwl07r0000gn/T/PyGitUp.2r0xiioq/master.issue-55/.git
	fetch = +refs/heads/*:refs/remotes/origin/*
[branch "feature#123"]

```

issue_title: Please provide a way to disable DirectorySandbox
issue_body:
setuptools now hardcode the restriction of file access within the directory of `setup.py`.  This makes out-of-source build impossible for setuptools, giving errors like:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "setup.py", line 103, in <module>

    return self.build_and_install(setup_script, setup_base)

So not everyone need Sandbox and please provide an easy way to disable it.

issue_title: The `--install-option` option in a requirements.txt file leaks to all later packages being installed
issue_body:
setuptools 28.3.0 contains a regression wherein if you ran `pip install -r requirements.txt` and the `requirements.txt` file made use of the `--install-option` feature like the following:

```
talon==1.3.2 --install-option="--no-ml"
cchardet==1.0.0
cssselect==0.9.2
lxml==3.6.1
regex==2016.7.21
```

setuptools would end up passing the `--no-ml` option to not just `talon` (in this case) but also all packages listed later in the requirements.txt file, giving errors like this:

```
  Running setup.py install for cchardet ... error
    Complete output from command /srv/zulip-venv-cache/8e84e610d0834b666d337a42b7cd5085b13331af/zulip-venv/bin/python -u -c "import setuptools, tokenize;__file__='/tmp/pip-build-9f_SEG/cchardet/setup.py';exec(compile(getattr(tokenize, 'open', open)(__file__).read().replace('\r\n', '\n'), __file__, 'exec'))" install --record /tmp/pip-JdIYOA-record/install-record.txt --single-version-externally-managed --compile --install-headers /srv/zulip-venv-cache/8e84e610d0834b666d337a42b7cd5085b13331af/zulip-venv/include/site/python2.7/cchardet --no-ml:
    usage: -c [global_opts] cmd1 [cmd1_opts] [cmd2 [cmd2_opts] ...]
       or: -c --help [cmd1 cmd2 ...]
       or: -c --help-commands
       or: -c cmd --help
    
    error: option --no-ml not recognized
    
    ----------------------------------------
Command "/srv/zulip-venv-cache/8e84e610d0834b666d337a42b7cd5085b13331af/zulip-venv/bin/python -u -c "import setuptools, tokenize;__file__='/tmp/pip-build-9f_SEG/cchardet/setup.py';exec(compile(getattr(tokenize, 'open', open)(__file__).read().replace('\r\n', '\n'), __file__, 'exec'))" install --record /tmp/pip-JdIYOA-record/install-record.txt --single-version-externally-managed --compile --install-headers /srv/zulip-venv-cache/8e84e610d0834b666d337a42b7cd5085b13331af/zulip-venv/include/site/python2.7/cchardet --no-ml" failed with error code 1 in /tmp/pip-build-9f_SEG/cchardet/
```

See https://github.com/zulip/zulip/issues/1959 for details.

I was able to fix the issue by downgrading to 27.3.1; I haven't bisected yet to see the precise version that introduced the issue, but I certainly can if that's helpful.

issue_title: Cython support should use new_build_ext
issue_body: In #1229 and [this comment](https://github.com/cython/cython/issues/2061#issuecomment-359071601), scoder recommendeds to prefer new_build_ext to build_ext.

issue_title: Vietnamese month uncertainty (discussion)
issue_body:
We need to come up with generic solution for this.

Vietnamese language does not have names for months and simply use "Month One", "Month Two" etc.
Some sites use numeric form like "Month 1", "Month 2" etc. So when we translate tokens from Vietnamese for dates like "1 Year 1 Month 1 Day" it is not quite clear whether it is "1 year 1 month 1 day" or "1 year 1 January day".

issue_title: Add support for IE to CSP middleware
issue_body:
When the user's browser is IE (NOT Edge),
the CSP header should be `X-Content-Security-Policy` instead of `Content-Security-Policy`.

Both headers can not be set at the same time, as it will cause unexpected behaviours on certain versions of browsers.

issue_title: Put Changes.rst updates in tag
issue_body:
Hiya!  This is just a pedantic request!  I've noticed that in the last two tags, the update to the CHANGES.rst for the changes in the tag arrived in a commit to master after the tag was made.  This confused me for 2.1.0, as I was only looking at the CHANGES.rst file for that tag, and wasn't sure what had made it in.

In the future, could you modify the CHANGES.rst file before that tag is made?  In my own projects, I've done this by updating the changelog in the commit that actually made a change.  E.g., you could maintain a 2.1.1 (SNAPSHOT) (or (ALPHA) or (WHATEVER)) tag at the top of CHANGES.rst, and then change it so it says the date of the tag when you make the tag.

Or do whatever you want! :)  Thank you!

issue_title: Deprecate build_sphinx in favor of build_docs
issue_body:
We currently have two commands to build the documentation that do the same thing (or, will once #5156 is merged):

    python setup.py build_docs
    python setup.py build_sphinx

Right now `AstropyBuildDocs` subclasses `AstropyBuildSphinx` and is really just an alias.  As discussed in astropy/astropy#5156, we could switch this so that `AstropyBuildDocs` is the primary command and add a deprecation warning to `AstropyBuildSphinx`.

This issue is paired with astropy/astropy#5157 - a changelog entry needs to be added to the main astropy repo for the deprecation, but the actual code needs to be changed here.

issue_title: Event signature check fails in Python 3.6.2
issue_body:
Apparently the issue is here:
https://github.com/stripe/stripe-python/blob/master/stripe/webhook.py#L59

I've been able to make it work with this change:

```python
        # signed_payload = "%d.%s" % (timestamp, payload)  # Doesn't work
        signed_payload = "%d.%s" % (timestamp, payload.decode('utf8'))  # works !
```

(payload is bytecode so `str(payload)` creates a representation of the bytecode (using stuff like `b\\` prefix))

issue_title: Update documentation todo list
Add overlap accessors to spatial_pooler.py
issue_body:
This issue is to add the following getter's to the Python SpatialPooler:

getOverlapScores
getBoostedOverlapScores

This would be implemented by adding self._overlaps and self._boostedOverlap member variables.  These should be initialized in the constructor to all zeros.

This will be a backward incompatible change, in that previously serialized spatial poolers will not load once this change has been made. As such, we should increment the VERSION flag in spatial_pooler.py and then in __setstate__() we should add the members in for older versions of the SP.

Tests should be added to ensure we are getting the correct results for overlap and boostedOverlap.

A corresponding change needs to be made to the C++ implementation, tracked here: https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/issues/974

>So, it depends which status you're getting back. The HTTP spec says that:
>
>- A 200 means that "The request has succeeded"
>- A 202 means that the request was accepted but not yet fulfilled;
>- A 204 means that "The server has fulfilled the request" but there is nothing to answer (that's what's usually used on DELETE operations)
>
>In cliquet, [it seems that we return a 200](https://github.com/mozilla-services/cliquet/blob/master/cliquet/resource/__init__.py#L499), which doesn't seem to be consistent with what you're describing here.

issue_title: RFC git branch reogranisation after the release of v1.2.0 and v2.0.0
issue_body:
After this week's two new feature releases, **v1.2.0** (stable) and **v2.0.0** (development), the time has come to forward our git branches again.

Here is the recall of our branch policy:

- [Understanding official repo branches](http://invenio.readthedocs.org/en/latest/developers/git-workflow.html#understanding-official-repo-branches)

The branch forwarding requires some extra conventions now that we have important technology differences between Invenio v1 and Invenio v2 code bases. Here is a plan we have been roughly thinking about:

**1.** There are two new release maintenance branches:

- maint-1.2
- maint-2.0

As usual, they accept only bug fixes, without any API changes or DB schema changes, etc.  This is not surprising, it works exactly like the usual `maint-X.Y` branches.

**2.** It is recommended to start any new developments on top of Invenio v2 code base only.  This means doing the following branch renaming:

- current `next` will be renamed to new `master`

All new developments will go here, and eventually this branch will give birth to a later release named v2.1, with its own future `maint-2.1` branch.  Again, nothing surprising here, this is exactly like the past `master` branches.

**3.** Some new features may still be developed for the old Invenio v1 code base.  (This is the novelty point, as in the past the `master` branch was simply forwarded.)  It means to perform the following branch renaming:

- current `master ` will be renamed to `old` or `legacy`

If an installation needs some new feature on top of their Invenio v1 instance, it may be developed there.  Eventually this might give birth to v1.3, v1.4, and even later v1.x code base releases.

However, and this is significant difference, if a new feature branch is prepared only for the "old" v1 codebase, without any counterpart for the "new" v2 codebase, then this feature won't get automatically merged upstream anymore, and might "die" inside some of the v1.x release series -- unless somebody takes it and adapts it for the new v2 code base.

This is dictated by the significant code infrastructure differences between Invenio v1 and v2, where it does not make much sense to develop new feature for v1 and try to "port it" to v2 later.

In summary, it is strongly recommended to use v2 code base for all new feature developments from now on, but if someone running an Invenio v1.2 instance needs a new feature, it is recommended to prepare two branches, one for v1.x and one for v2.x codebase, otherwise the feature may die inside some of the v1.x releases.

**4.** What about past feature requests or pull requests?

The triage team will reclassify past issues, feature requests, and pull requests as either for the "old" v1 code base (when important to a service), or the "new" v2 code base (preferred), or for the "old and new" code bases (rare).  Such a triage effort has been running in the past few months and it will intensify now that v2.0.0 is out.

**5.** What about unstable features that needs side maturing?

Again, we can use `next` and `pu` branches, as until now, which means that:

- current `pu` will be dropped
- we can start new `next` out of `master` when a need arises
- we can start new `pu` out of `next` when a need arises

As in the past, it is recommended to use `master` for all new feature developments whenever possible, with a commit-early-commit-often policy, all the while carefully isolating potentially dangerous or immature features by making them easily "unpluggable" or otherwise "configurable away" from the setup, in order not to hurt other existing Invenio v2 installations.  See items 4 and 5 of our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.rst).

**WDYT?** Especially those teams that may need have new non-trivial feature developments for the old v1.x code base in the pipeline?

issue_title: api: impossibility to use multiple elasticsearch indexes
issue_body:
**Problem:**
The only way to index records in multiple indexes is to create a custom `RecordIndexer` and give it a custom  `record_to_index` function.

The CLI and tasks recreate the `RecordIndexer` instances at each call. Thus they will always use the default `record_to_index`. Thus it is not possible to create custom `RecordIndexer` as the CLI would behave differently.

**Possible Solutions:**
1/ Have only one `RecordIndexer`.
- Add a config variable which can point to an alternative `RecordIndexer` class. The CLI should use it.
- Make the indexing functions static.
- Remove customization arguments from the constructor and add them as class attributes.

Effect on other projects:
- invenio-deposit: need to remove [the creation of a `RecordIndexer`](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-deposit/blob/master/invenio_deposit/api.py#L115)

2/ Allow multiple `RecordIndexer`.
- Register a dict of `RecordIndexer` on the InvenioIndexer extension.
- Add a parameter to the CLI which enables to choose which `RecordIndexer` should be used.

Problem: How do we know which record should be indexed with which `RecordIndexer`?

issue_title: Add summary (with Terms of Use) for each geocoder to docs
issue_body:
(This issue is originating from the current roadmap #267).

The goal is to have an informative summary which would help choosing a suitable geocoder service.

I tend to think of this as a separate section at [geopy Read The Docs](http://geopy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) which is included from a separate `.rst` file between [`geopy.geocoders.__doc__` and `geopy.geocoders.get_geocoder_for_service`](https://github.com/geopy/geopy/blob/67a18801cc87eec40e1f1e8edaa6f297228be91e/docs/index.rst#geocoders). 

I don't know about the representation yet. Table would be very informative, however it might become too large and thus unreadable.

Here are some examples from the similar libraries for inspiration:
- [alexreisner/geocoder](https://github.com/alexreisner/geocoder#street-address-services)
- [geocoder-php/Geocoder](https://github.com/geocoder-php/Geocoder#providers)
- [DenisCarriere/geocoder](https://github.com/DenisCarriere/geocoder#providers)

My suggestions on what to include in this summary for each geocoding service:
- Is API key required
- Are paid plans available (just a URL will do, I believe)
- Free usage limits + rate limits (with a URL)
- Restrictions on data (can/must be cached/stored)
- Ability to do forward/reverse geocoding (batch geocoding is not supported by geopy yet, so we can leave this for now)
- Support for geometries in responses in any form (e.g. geojson/wkt or just a bounding box).
- Is there a need to pass custom User-Agent/Referer.
- Is there a need to display attribution
- Territory coverage

cc @umbe1987 who [was willing to help with this](https://github.com/geopy/geopy/issues/267#issuecomment-374880607)

If parameter name in the query does not match with any key in the parameter value object. Getting this error "Did you forget to apply '|inclause' to your query?"
Angular size units
issue_body:
GalSim parameters like FWHM and half-light-radius that specify transverse sizes are generally treated as dimensionless, with the implicit assumption that the user is free to work in arcsec, microrad, microns, etc, although most (all?) examples use arcsecs.  However, the Airy, Kolmogorov and OpticalPSF profiles are not scale free so there is some potential confusion.  Specifically, the Airy.getFWHM and Kolmogorov.getFWHM return sizes in radians, which is probably not what the user is expecting if they are consistently using arcsecs for their galaxy profile sizes.  This confusion is probably not helped by the following docstring for the Image ctor scale parameter:

        scale       You can optionally specify a pixel scale to use.  This would normally have
                    units arcsec/pixel, but it doesn't have to be arcsec.  If you want to
                    use different units for the physical scale of your galsim objects, then
                    the same unit would be used here.

The simplest solution is probably to just clearly document that the Airy and Kolmogorov profiles work in radians, but that will still trip  someone up who makes reasonable assumptions and doesn't read the docs carefully.

A more radical solution would be to require explicit units on all size and scale parameters so that inconsistent units generate runtime errors, e.g. using astropy.units (which I didn't like at first, but is growing on me).

- [√] This is a single icon or matched pair (Ex: `lock` / `unlock`)
- [√] The title starts with "Icon request: " and is followed by the requested icon name (Ex: `Icon request: magical-unicorn`)
- [√] I have [searched for existing issues](https://github.com/FortAwesome/Font-Awesome/issues) and to the best of my knowledge this is not a duplicate
- [√] I have included at least one example image if it could be helpful (optional)
- [√] The request is for a concrete object (it's harder to make an icon to represent happiness, it's easier to make a smiley face. ☺)

issue_title: Feels like we've added a bit more complexity to the datastore API, comments?
issue_body:
Hi guys,

I was reviewing the "state of the world" of the datastore API, and found a few things that seem (to me at least) a bit more complicated than they should be.

A bit of this comes from the authentication stuff (which we have another open bug for) but I wanted to bring up a few things for discussion... If this issue gets out of control, we can move the discussion elsewhere, but I wanted to kick things off:

### Create a new Person entity in the Datastore

#### What I have to write

```python
from gcloud import datastore
datastore.set_defaults()
entity = datastore.Entity(key=datastore.Key('Person'))
entity.update({'name': 'Jimmy'})
datastore.put([entity])
```

#### What I'd like to write

```python
from gcloud import datastore
entity = datastore.Entity('Person')
entity.update({'name': 'Jimmy'})
entity.put()
```

#### Key differences

1. no `set_defaults()` (there's another issue open for this I believe)
1. Create an entity of kind Person without lots more typing (or maybe we get the entity with `datastore.Kind('Person').get_entity()` ?)
1. `.put` not requiring a list for a single entity

### Get an existing Person (123) from the Datastore

#### What I have to write

```python
from gcloud import datastore
datastore.set_defaults()
entity = datastore.get(datastore.Key('Person', 123))
```

#### What I'd like to write

```python
from gcloud import datastore
datastore.set_defaults()
entity = datastore.Kind('Person').get_by_id(123)
```

#### Key differences

1. No `set_defaults`
1. Equivalent of "drilling down" into a "table" (kind), and then a "row" (id)

_(Not hugely concerned about this use case, but wanted to toss it out)_

### Create a new Person using a specific set of credentials (`/home/creds.json`) to talk to a specific dataset (`'my-dataset'`) in the Datastore

(Being looked into in PR https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-python/pull/641)

#### What I have to write

_(This is a best guess... I don't know if this code is even right... And even so, this code won't do the same thing if you ran it on App Engine because of the order of evaluation here: http://googlecloudplatform.github.io/gcloud-python/latest/gcloud-api.html#gcloud.credentials.get_credentials )_

```python
import sys
sys.env['GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS'] = '/home/creds.json'

from gcloud import datastore
datastore.set_defaults()
entity = datastore.Entity(key=datastore.Key('Person', dataset_id='my-dataset'))
entity.update{'name': 'Jimmy'})
datastore.put(entity)
```

#### What I'd like to write

```python
from gcloud import datastore
datastore.set_credentials('/home/creds.json')
dataset = datastore.Dataset('my-dataset')
entity = dataset.Entity('Person')
entity.update({'name': 'Jimmy'})
entity.put()
```

#### Key differences

1. Simple in-code way to set which credentials file to use
1. High-level concept of a dataset
1. Datasets can provide Entities just like `datastore` would

/cc @tseaver @dhermes @pcostell

issue_title: Milestones for 2015?
issue_body:
Hi guys,

Now that 2015 is here, I was wondering if we could take another pass at the milestones. We originally went with "let's just try to break this down by different services" and that seemed to kick things off. I don't know if that's the same right now, given we have the majority of work done for the different services, and still have some finishing touches.

Could we brainstorm some good milestones for the year and then update them in here?

### Straw man for us to discuss

- `gcloud.datastore` 1.0 (any issue related to Datastore getting to 1.0 from now on would go here)
- NDB Integrated into Datastore (I suspect there will be a lot of issues related to this -- maybe this is a sub-milestone to the above?)
- `gcloud.storage` 1.0 (include getting Google's Cloud Storage team to give us a :+1: on this)
- Documentation 1.0 (anything that's needed to get our docs being what we consider to be 1.0 "done")
- `gcloud.pubsub` 0.1 (anything that we need to do to get pubsub to an "alpha" that works but might be backwards incompatible over time)
- `gcloud.pubsub` 0.5 (get to "beta" -- works, stable, less likely to be backwards incompatible, but still useful)
- `gcloud.pubsub` 1.0 (including sign-off from Google's Cloud Pub/Sub team)

(same for DNS, BigQuery, SQL, and Compute)

Thoughts?

### Straw man of service priorities

1. Datastore
1. Storage
1. Pub/Sub
1. DNS
1. BigQuery
1. SQL
1. Compute

### Proposed "milestones" to add to gcloud-python for our 2015 work

1. datastore stable
1. datastore future
1. storage stable
1. storage future
1. pubsub alpha
1. pubsub beta
1. pubsub stable
1. pubsub future
1. dns alpha
1. dns beta
1. dns stable
1. dns future

and a "catch all" called "stable" for things that aren't specific to a service.

/cc @dhermes @tseaver

1. OS type and version

Linux (appengine flexible python runtime)

issue_title: Storage: error on HTTP 206 Partial Content discards message leading to confusing errors
issue_labels: [{'id': 958354227, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw5NTgzNTQyMjc=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/:rotating_light:', 'name': ':rotating_light:', 'color': 'FFFFFF', 'default': False}, {'id': 77678602, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw3NzY3ODYwMg==', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20storage', 'name': 'api: storage', 'color': 'f8f8f8', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604243, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw1NDQ2MDQyNDM=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p2', 'name': 'priority: p2', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 778245707, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw3NzgyNDU3MDc=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/status:%20awaiting%20information', 'name': 'status: awaiting information', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438580, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw3NzQzODU4MA==', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20bug', 'name': 'type: bug', 'color': 'db4437', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
In `download_to_file`,[ if the download code raises `resumable_media.InvalidResponse` [1], it calls `_raise_from_invalid_response`](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/storage/google/cloud/storage/blob.py#L499), which calls `exceptions.from_http_response`, which [creates a new exception using the HTTP response inside the `resumable_media.InvalidResponse`](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/core/google/api/core/exceptions.py#L386), which discards any exception message that was put there. This leads to confusing exceptions like the following (full stack trace below):

```
google.api.core.exceptions.GoogleAPICallError
206 GET https://www.googleapis.com/download/storage/v1/b/bucket_name/o/object_name?alt=media: ��eC�uXS@�..."
```

This shows a 206 SUCCESS code, but apparently it still failed for some reason. Digging through the code shows a number of reasons this can happen, such as [if the response body length does not match the Content-Length header](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-resumable-media-python/blob/master/google/resumable_media/_download.py#L353).

I'm not entirely sure how to fix this, but I would be willing to contribute a PR if someone can suggest a solution. My suggestion would be to change `_raise_from_invalid_response` to replace the message on the exception that is raised with the message from the `InvalidResponse` object itself (maybe formatted with `''.join(str(s for s in e.args))`).

*Details*
*OS type and version*: Linux
*Python version*: Python 2.7.13
*google-cloud-python version*: 1.4.0 (but code inspection shows this still exists):
```
google-cloud-core==0.27.1
google-cloud-datastore==1.3.0
google-cloud-storage==1.4.0
```

*Stacktrace*:
```
File "app_code.py", line 123, in some_function
  src_blob.download_to_file(src_temp)
File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/cloud/storage/blob.py", line 466, in download_to_file
  _raise_from_invalid_response(exc)
File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/cloud/storage/blob.py", line 1605, in _raise_from_invalid_response
  raise exceptions.from_http_response(error.response)
```

*Steps to reproduce*

* Somehow get a GCS download to return a 206 code then truncate the response. I don't actually know how to reproduce this.

issue_title: `bootstrap_pex_env` should support `PEX_PATH`
issue_body:
currently, using `pex_bootstrapper.bootstrap_pex_env()` to bootstrap imports from an external pex inside of a running python intepreter does not appear to natively handle `PEX_PATH`:

```
[omerta xxx]$ pex requests -o requests.pex
[omerta xxx]$ pex flask -o flask.pex
[omerta xxx]$ python2.7
Python 2.7.13 (default, Mar  2 2017, 16:22:01) 
[GCC 4.2.1 Compatible Apple LLVM 8.0.0 (clang-800.0.42.1)] on darwin
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> import os, sys
>>> pex_filename = 'flask.pex'
>>> sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(os.path.abspath(pex_filename), '.bootstrap'))
>>> from _pex import pex_bootstrapper
>>> os.environ['PEX_PATH'] = os.path.abspath('./requests.pex')
>>> pex_bootstrapper.bootstrap_pex_env(pex_filename)
>>> import requests

but it'd be nice if this "just worked" in the same fashion of the typical pex runtime entrypoint.

issue_title: junit synthetic classpath does not generate properly escaped MANIFEST.MF
issue_body:
For example, a file path like
```
$ ls org/apache/pig/pig/0.13.0%2B38/pig-0.13.0%2B38-h2.jar
org/apache/pig/pig/0.13.0%2B38/pig-0.13.0%2B38-h2.jar
```
will appear as is in `MANIFEST.MF`

Whereas JVM classloader treats it as a uri and tries to decode it to `org/apache/pig/pig/0.13.0+38/pig-0.13.0+38-h2.jar`, which is not valid anymore, causing ClassNotFound. 

The proper path to be in MANIFEST.MF is:
```
org/apache/pig/pig/0.13.0%252B38/pig-0.13.0%252B38-h2.jar
```
which gets decoded to `org/apache/pig/pig/0.13.0%2B38/pig-0.13.0%2B38-h2.jar`

issue_title: `--no-compile-zinc-incremental` does not purge existing classfiles
issue_body: `--no-incremental` causes target analysis to be empty each time. But it does not also purge the output classes directory, so it means that non-incremental compiles can still suffer from issues like https://github.com/sbt/zinc/issues/144 (which seem to be caused by the classfiles already being present on disk).

issue_title: The junit runner should support forked execution
issue_body: Right now, the only recourse to run tests that are ill behaved or use ill-behaved infrastructure is to use `./pants test.junit --batch-size=1 ...' to effect a jvm-per-test.  This has the major downside of also running tests serially.  There should be a way to turn on batch parallization, ie respect the `--parallel-threads` while also turning on batch size 1, aka a forked execution model.

issue_title: Add info about local use codes
issue_body:
Some three-letter codes are [reserved for local use](http://www-01.sil.org/iso639-3/scope.asp#R) by ISO 639-3. This information should be available programmatically, e.g. as a list:
```python
from string import ascii_lowercase
local_use = ['q' + x + y for x in ascii_lowercase[:ascii_lowercase.index('t') + 1] for y in ascii_lowercase]
```

issue_title: Stemming feminine nouns in german stemmer

issue_title: Blind increment on Counter column
issue_body:
As far as I could tell it's not possible to increment a counter column by a specified amount.  Instead, the counter value must be explicitly set.  The CounterUpdateClause simply takes the supplied value and subtracts the previous value.  I believe that an update should be able to be called on a counter column without knowing the previous value.  Something along the lines of

MyModel.objects.filter(id=1).update(counter_col__increment=1)

which result in the clause: 
"counter_col" = "counter_col" + 1

-or-

MyModel.objects.filter(id=1).update(counter_col__decrement=1)

which would result in the clause:
"counter_col" = "counter_col" - 1

In my specific use case, I usually don't care what the actual value of the counter is.  I just need to increment it.  It saves a query on the database.  This would also keep the counter thread safe since there is no guarantee that the "previous" parameter supplied to ContainerUpdateClause.__init__ is fresh.

http://www.datastax.com/documentation/cql/3.0/cql/cql_using/use_counter_t.html

issue_title: Valid commands being read as invalid
'ContinuousFactor' object has no attribute 'variables'
issue_body:
### Subject of the issue
Belief Propagation doesn't work with continuous factor

### Your environment
* pgmpy version   0.1.6
* Python version   3.6 with spyder
* Operating System  Win7

### Steps to reproduce
Tell us how to reproduce this issue. Please provide the complete code if possible.
I ran the program:
-----------------------------------------------------
from pgmpy.models import FactorGraph
from pgmpy.factors.continuous import ContinuousFactor
from scipy.stats import multivariate_normal
G = FactorGraph()
G.add_nodes_from(['a', 'b'])
normal_pdf = lambda a, b: multivariate_normal.pdf((a, b), [0, 0], [[1, 0], [0, 1]])
phi1 = ContinuousFactor(['a', 'b'], normal_pdf)
G.add_node(phi1)
G.add_factors(phi1)
from pgmpy.inference import BeliefPropagation 
bp =  BeliefPropagation(G)
-----------------------------------------------------

### Expected behaviour
Tell us what should happen
no error

### Actual behaviour
Tell us what happens instead
The program throws an error:
" AttributeError: 'ContinuousFactor' object has no attribute 'variables'  "

The same error comes up with VariableElimination instead of  BeliefPropagation.
With discrete variables I don't get this error.
Are there any inference methods that work with continuous variables?
Are continuous variables really supported by pgmpy? In documentation/tutorials I see lots of examples with discrete variables, but hardly any with continuous ones.

issue_body:
The Alberta Electric System Operator (AESO) has legislative obligations to make their data public. You can get a sense for some of it at: http://ets.aeso.ca/

It's rough, but CSV data is available at: http://ets.aeso.ca/ets_web/ip/Market/Reports/CSDReportServlet?contentType=csv
